title: Active Theme — CSS Color Class Reference
topic: Live reference for the currently loaded theme pack — every painted surface, mapped to design tokens
summary: Live, dynamic reference for the currently loaded theme pack. Every swatch resolves at render time from the document's CSS custom properties, so switching themes re-paints the badges automatically. Each row carries a stable ID tag (R4.SECTION.N) for direct reference.
content:
Overview

An expanded accordion-style reference of every CSS class that paints color, resolved against whatever theme pack is currently loaded. Each color swatch in the table is dynamic — it reads from the document's active CSS custom properties at render time, so switching themes (avatar menu → palette icon, or Theme Settings) repaints the badges live. The default theme is provided by app/globals.css; theme packs in public/themes/<slug>.css override individual tokens via the FOUC-prevention <head> snippet in app/layout.tsx.

Every row carries a stable ID tag (e.g. R4.ZTB.4) so you can quote it back in a single token to change a color or rule. Section headings carry the parent ID (e.g. R4.ZTB). Hex and rgba badges are rendered in the actual color they apply.

Section index

  • R4.LIVE — Live working example — interactive accordion
  • R4.PAL — Active palette
  • R4.FND — Strategy Zone — Foundation tokens (:root)
  • R4.STA — Strategy Zone — Status tokens

R4.LIVELive working example — interactive accordion

This section uses real CSS classes from the codebase, so colors update automatically when the user switches themes via the avatar menu → palette icon. The hex-value tables further down are frozen snapshots — switch themes and watch which renders update vs which don't.

R4.LIVE.1Tables

Tables

Body cells use --ink background with --ink-contrast text. Header uses --surface-sunken. Row hover paints --accent.

Zone-toggle accordion — layers-editor pattern

The TABLES band below is a real .layers-editor__zone-toggle button (the same component used in the portfolio-model layers editor). The chevron rotates and the inner rows show/hide on click.

Plain table — default CSS

A bare <table class="table"> with no zone-toggle wrapping. This is what you get with default styling: .table-wrap outer chrome, .table__head header band, .table__row body rows with hover, .table__cell cells.

How the theme is loaded

  1. The user picks the active theme on /theme; the choice writes localStorage['vector-theme-pack'] = the slug of the active theme pack.
  2. On every subsequent load, the inline FOUC script in app/layout.tsx reads that key and injects <link rel="stylesheet" href="/themes/<active-theme>.css" id="vector-theme-pack"> before <body> renders.
  3. The stylesheet's :root block overrides foundation tokens defined in app/globals.css; the element-level rules (tables, buttons, sidebar items, sidebar borders, zone-toggle bands) override globals.css selectors that consume var(--surface-sunken), var(--ink), etc.
  4. Reverting to Default theme deletes the link element and removes the localStorage key — globals.css tokens take effect alone.

Sources

  1. Internal — public/themes/<active-theme>.css (the theme stylesheet documented here)
  2. Internal — app/globals.css (default tokens + table / button / sidebar / zone-toggle base rules being overridden)
  3. Internal — app/layout.tsx (FOUC-prevention <head> snippet that injects the link tag)
  4. Internal — app/(user)/theme/page.tsx (theme picker UI; swatches [--accent, --ink, --canvas, --surface-sunken])
  5. Internal — app/(user)/portfolio-model/LayersTable.tsx (zone-toggle JSX whose color this theme paints)

Gaps

  • This reference covers color only. Spacing, typography, and shadow tokens for the active theme are inherited from globals.css unchanged and are not enumerated here.
  • The dark slot is defined in the stylesheet but not yet wired through the theme picker UI — it activates only if some other code path sets data-theme="dark" on the root.
  • Hover-state coverage is limited to .btn, .sidebar-item, and table rows. Other globals.css interactive surfaces (inputs, chips, pill close buttons) keep their default-theme hover behaviour.
